title: Hermes JavaScript Engine Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Hermes, AOT compilation, and its impact on React Native performance.

questions.question: What fundamental difference distinguishes Hermes from JavaScriptCore (JSC) in how it processes JavaScript?

options.text: Hermes uses a multi-threaded approach while JSC is single-threaded
Hermes compiles JavaScript to bytecode at build time (AOT), while JSC compiles at runtime (JIT), reducing app startup cost
Hermes runs JavaScript natively without compilation; JSC always interprets it
Hermes only runs on Android; JSC runs on both Android and iOS

questions.question: How do you verify that Hermes is the active JavaScript engine at runtime in React Native?

options.text: Check if navigator.userAgent includes 'Hermes'
Check if `global.HermesInternal` is defined — it only exists when Hermes is active
Call `Platform.getEngine()` from react-native
Read the engine name from `process.env.JS_ENGINE`

questions.question: Which app performance metric is most significantly improved by Hermes's AOT bytecode approach?

options.text: Frame rate during animations (FPS)
Network request latency
Time to Interactive (TTI) / app startup time — bytecode loads directly without compile time
Battery consumption during idle backgrounding

questions.question: A third-party library breaks your app after switching to Hermes. What is the most likely cause?

options.text: Hermes does not support TypeScript
The library uses eval() or new Function(), which Hermes does not support for security reasons
Hermes requires all imports to use CommonJS require() syntax
The library uses async/await which is not supported by Hermes

questions.question: How do you enable Hermes in an Expo managed workflow project (SDK 48+)?

options.text: Add `hermes: true` to the `ios` section of app.json
Install the `hermes-engine` npm package and import it in App.js
Set `"jsEngine": "hermes"` in the expo section of app.json — it is already the default in SDK 48+
Edit the metro.config.js to use the hermes transformer
